CTA on Image Gallery — Experiment Readout

Cars for Sale · Android + Mobile Web · Launch 23 Jun (Android) / 29 Jun (MWeb) 2026 · Data through 5 Jul 2026
Overall TLDR
Android
Mobile Web
Bottom line: The in-gallery contact CTA is a win on both platforms — it lifts Browser→Responder and grows total leads significantly, with clean gallery-browsing guardrails. Leads are net incremental on both (more so on Android). We will continue monitoring both platforms for ~2 more weeks and resolve the Android SRM flag before the final ship call.

Platform comparison

MetricAndroid (8d · 6/28–7/05)Mobile Web (7d · 6/29–7/05)
Browser → Responder+11.3% sig+13.5% sig
Leads / user+6.2% sig+15.5% sig
Leads / adview+4.6% sig+16.2% sig
SRM (split health)49.67% · p=0.0005 flag50.11% · p=0.21 ok
% leads via gallery CTA7.2%21.7%
Net incremental leads+740 (~81% net-new)+2,226 (~62% net-new)
Gallery open rate (guardrail)flat · p=0.18 okflat · p=0.98 ok
Trendstable ~+11–12%stable ~+13.5%

Recommendation

Positive read on both platforms. Mobile Web is the stronger and cleaner result (bigger lead lift, no SRM). Android is also a clear win but carries a minor SRM flag to resolve. Both show durable, non-decaying trends and clean gallery guardrails.

Next steps: Continue monitoring both platforms for ~2 more weeks to confirm the lift holds before the final ship call. Target checkpoint: ~19 July 2026.

1Problem statement & objective

Buyers on the Cars for Sale adview browse through the image gallery but, in the control experience, must exit the gallery and return to the adview page to contact the seller. Every gallery swipe is a moment of high buying intent, but the contact action lives outside that flow.

Hypothesis: placing a contact CTA directly inside the image gallery captures that intent at its peak and converts more browsers into responders — without disrupting how people browse images. Objective: increase Browser→Responder and grow total leads on Android Cars for Sale.

2Success metrics

Primary: Browser → Responder conversion (responders ÷ browsers). Secondary: leads per adview, per user, per browser, per responder. Guardrail: gallery open rate & opens per opener — the CTA must not disrupt image browsing.

3Experiment setup

Platform / CategoryAndroid · Cars for Sale
Launch23 June 2026 (~1 week ramp to 100%)
Split50 / 50, user-level
Data window28 June – 5 July 2026 (8 days)
Control — gallery photos only; user exits to the adview to contact.
Treatment — listing title + price header and a pinned Chat / Call / WhatsApp bar inside the gallery.

4How the data was analyzed

Scoped to Android · Cars for Sale (other platforms/categories excluded as out of scope). Aggregated to period totals per arm. Two-proportion z-tests for rate metrics, chi-squared for SRM, 95% CI on the absolute difference. Leads-per-responder is a ratio, reported descriptively. 28 June is noted as a partial-rollout (ramp) day.

5Data sanity check

Control 138,372 users vs Treatment 136,553 users over 8 days — large, well-powered sample.

⚠️ SRM flag: treatment ran at 49.67% of traffic (expected 50%), chi-squared p=0.0005. The shortfall is small (~0.3pp) and stable day-to-day, pointing to bucketing/eligibility logic rather than broken randomization. Confirm the cause with data/eng before final readout.
⚠️ Metric definition note: the leads_gallery_openers field was revised upward between the 3-day and 8-day pulls. Lock the canonical definition with the data team.

6A/B test results

MetricControlTreatmentLiftp-valueSig
Browser → Responder3.70%4.12%+11.3%<0.0001sig
Leads / user8.81%9.35%+6.2%<0.0001sig
Leads / adview0.858%0.898%+4.6%0.0003sig
Leads / responder2.382.27−4.6%—ratio

Daily Browser→Responder lift

Control vs Treatment

Trend

Cumulative Browser→Responder lift stabilizes at ~+11–12% after the ramp day and stays significant every day through 5 July — a durable effect, not a decaying novelty spike.

Feature engagement

Gallery CTA: 869 clickers / 1,036 clicks → 914 leads via the CTA (7.2% of all treatment leads).

Guardrail ✅: gallery open rate flat (46.5% vs 46.8%, p=0.18); opens per opener identical (4.04 vs 4.05). The CTA does not suppress image browsing.

Incrementality — does it add leads or just shift them?

Net incremental leads
+740
vs control lead-rate
Leads via gallery CTA
914
7.2% of leads
Shifted (cannibalized)
~174
~19% of CTA volume
Net-new share
~81%
genuinely incremental
Predominantly incremental. Total leads rose to 12,773 vs ~12,033 expected at the control rate. Non-gallery-CTA leads per user were nearly unchanged (−1.4%, not significant, p=0.24) — other contact paths were not meaningfully cannibalized. ~4 of every 5 gallery-CTA leads are net-new demand.
How is incrementality calculated? (methodology)

The gallery CTA is a new place to submit a lead, so leads flowing through it aren't automatically new demand — some users would have contacted the seller anyway via Chat/Call/WhatsApp and simply used the new button instead. Incrementality separates net-new leads from shifted ones. We use two cross-checking methods plus a confirmation test.

Method 1 — Expected vs actual (counterfactual). Control gives the natural lead rate with no feature: 8.81% leads per user. If treatment behaved the same, it would produce that rate across its 136,553 users → ~12,033 expected leads. Treatment actually produced 12,773. The difference is the net gain the feature caused:

12,773 − 12,033 = +740 incremental leads

This is measured on total leads, so it already nets out any shifting.

Method 2 — Compare to CTA volume (split new vs shifted). The data shows 914 leads came through the gallery CTA. If all were brand-new, total leads would have risen by the full 914. They only rose by 740, so the gap is leads that used the CTA instead of a path they'd have used anyway:

914 − 740 = ~174 shifted  →  net-new share ≈ 81%

Confirmation — did other paths actually drop? If leads were pulled from other channels, non-CTA leads should fall. Stripping out CTA leads: non-CTA leads/user = 8.69% (treatment) vs 8.81% (control) = −1.4%, not statistically significant.

Note: this assumes control's lead rate is a valid counterfactual, i.e. the arms are comparable. We normalise by rate per user (not raw counts) so any small headcount imbalance between arms doesn't distort the math.

7Recommended next actions

Positive early read — all success metrics significant and aligned, guardrails clean, lead growth genuinely incremental.

Next step: continue monitoring Android for ~2 more weeks to confirm the ~+11% cumulative lift stays stable, then make the ship call. Next checkpoint: ~19 July 2026.

1Problem statement & objective

Buyers on the Cars for Sale adview browse through the image gallery but, in the control experience, must exit the gallery and return to the adview page to contact the seller. Every gallery swipe is a moment of high buying intent, but the contact action lives outside that flow.

Hypothesis: placing a contact CTA directly inside the image gallery captures that intent at its peak and converts more browsers into responders — without disrupting how people browse images. Objective: increase Browser→Responder and grow total leads on Mobile Web Cars for Sale.

2Success metrics

Primary: Browser → Responder conversion. Secondary: leads per adview, per user, per browser, per responder. Guardrail: gallery open rate & opens per opener.

3Experiment setup

Platform / CategoryMobile Web · Cars for Sale
Launch29 June 2026
Split50 / 50, user-level
Data window29 June – 5 July 2026 (7 days)
Control — gallery photos only; user exits to the adview to contact.
Treatment — listing title + price header and a pinned Chat / Call / WhatsApp bar inside the gallery.

4How the data was analyzed

Scoped to Mobile Web · Cars for Sale. Aggregated to period totals per arm. Two-proportion z-tests for rate metrics, chi-squared for SRM, 95% CI on absolute difference. Leads-per-responder reported descriptively.

5Data sanity check

Control 172,477 users vs Treatment 173,213 users over 7 days.

✅ SRM passes: treatment at 50.11% of traffic, chi-squared p=0.21. Clean split — no imbalance concern.

6A/B test results

MetricControlTreatmentLiftp-valueSig
Browser → Responder4.34%4.93%+13.5%<0.0001sig
Leads / user8.30%9.58%+15.5%<0.0001sig
Leads / adview2.15%2.50%+16.2%<0.0001sig
Leads / responder1.911.95+1.8%—ratio

Daily Browser→Responder lift

Control vs Treatment

Trend

Cumulative Browser→Responder lift stabilizes at ~+13.5% and is significant from day 1. No decay.

Feature engagement

Gallery CTA: 2,632 clickers / 3,811 clicks → 3,600 leads via the CTA (21.7% of all treatment leads) — much higher CTA reliance than Android.

Guardrail ✅: gallery open rate flat (50.50% both arms, p=0.98); opens per opener identical (3.98). No disruption to browsing.

Incrementality — does it add leads or just shift them?

Net incremental leads
+2,226
vs control lead-rate
Leads via gallery CTA
3,600
21.7% of leads
Shifted (cannibalized)
~1,374
~38% of CTA volume
Net-new share
~62%
genuinely incremental
Net-positive, but more shifting than Android. Total leads rose to 16,602 vs ~14,376 expected. The CTA captured 3,600 leads while the net gain was +2,226, implying ~1,374 shifted from other paths. Non-gallery-CTA leads per user dropped −9.6% (significant, p<0.0001) — other contact paths were meaningfully cannibalized. Still ~6 of 10 gallery-CTA leads are net-new.
How is incrementality calculated? (methodology)

The gallery CTA is a new place to submit a lead, so leads flowing through it aren't automatically new demand — some users would have contacted the seller anyway via Chat/Call/WhatsApp and simply used the new button instead. Incrementality separates net-new leads from shifted ones. We use two cross-checking methods plus a confirmation test.

Method 1 — Expected vs actual (counterfactual). Control gives the natural lead rate with no feature: 8.30% leads per user. If treatment behaved the same, it would produce that rate across its 173,213 users → ~14,376 expected leads. Treatment actually produced 16,602. The difference is the net gain the feature caused:

16,602 − 14,376 = +2,226 incremental leads

This is measured on total leads, so it already nets out any shifting.

Method 2 — Compare to CTA volume (split new vs shifted). The data shows 3,600 leads came through the gallery CTA. If all were brand-new, total leads would have risen by the full 3,600. They only rose by 2,226, so the gap is leads that used the CTA instead of a path they'd have used anyway:

3,600 − 2,226 = ~1,374 shifted  →  net-new share ≈ 62%

Confirmation — did other paths actually drop? If leads were pulled from other channels, non-CTA leads should fall. Stripping out CTA leads: non-CTA leads/user = 7.51% (treatment) vs 8.30% (control) = −9.6%, statistically significant.

Note: this assumes control's lead rate is a valid counterfactual, i.e. the arms are comparable. We normalise by rate per user (not raw counts) so any small headcount imbalance between arms doesn't distort the math.

7Recommended next actions

Strongest and cleanest result of the two — significant across all metrics, no SRM issue, durable trend.

Next step: continue monitoring Mobile Web for ~2 more weeks to confirm durability, then make the ship call. Next checkpoint: ~19 July 2026.

Analysis scope: Android & Mobile Web · Cars for Sale. Source: BigQuery extract (CTA on Image Gallery Data).
